mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate crossing above 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in 70 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 55 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of observed cases, or interaction term IC is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration during tightening periods yields negative 20-day forward returns in over 65 percent of cases, or the mechanism shows zero predictive power when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index values signal inflationary pressures that force markets to price in tighter future monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases consistently coincide with positive 5-day forward returns, or the correlation coefficient remains above zero across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls additions dropping below 100 thousand indicate severe labor market contraction that triggers recession fears, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in 70 percent of months where PAYEMS growth is under 100 thousand, or the threshold signal shows no statistical edge.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly personal consumption expenditures reveals demand fragility that causes equity sell-offs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: Positive divergence between GDPC1 and RSAFS consistently yields positive 20-day forward returns, or the interaction effect fails to improve predictive accuracy over baseline models.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals supply chain bottlenecks that constrain corporate earnings, generating negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during recovery phases produce positive 5-day forward returns in over 60 percent of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when supply constraints are absent.
